author: mark
text:
review: never deliver .balls runtime paths; rewind main on failure

add_user_changes stages worktree changes and then git-rm --cached the runtime paths (.balls/local, .balls/tasks, .balls/worktree), so a stale .gitignore can't smuggle them into the squash. commit_squash_and_flip and a rewind-on-error guard wrap the integration-branch mutation: if any step between the squash landing and the state-branch flip fails, main is reset (working tree) or update-ref'd (bare gitdir) back to its pre-review tip. commit_touches_runtime is the post-squash backstop in case the staging filter is ever bypassed.

Reproduces a user incident where a failed bl review left a squash commit on master that contained the runtime symlinks and bricked bl until manual cleanup.
